name: Apple Juice Roast
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
Sweet potatoes should be pared and cut into pieces.
Cook onions in 2 tablespoons butter or shortening in a Dutch oven until tender-crisp; set aside.
Brown roast in remaining butter or shortening in the Dutch oven over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes, or until browned on both sides. Pierce entire surface of meat with a fork.
Combine apple juice, ketchup, salt, pepper, thyme, mustard, and basil; add to meat.
Top meat with reserved cooked onions; cover and cook slowly for 2 1/2 hours, or until almost tender.
Brush sweet potatoes with lemon juice for bright color; add to meat. Continue cooking, covered, for 30 to 40 minutes, or until meat and potatoes are tender.
Place meat and potatoes on a warm platter. Sprinkle potatoes with chopped parsley or garnish with apple rings and parsley if desired. Serve gravy over sliced meat.
NOTE:
After the apple juice mixture is added to browned meat, it may be marinated in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours before serving time. Turn the meat several times.
If using a cast iron Dutch oven, transfer contents to a glass dish.
GRAVY: Skim excess fat from cooking liquid; add water if needed to make 1 1/2 cups. Mix 1/2 cup water and 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour; stir gradually into cooking liquid. Heat to boiling; cook, stirring for 3 to 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper if desired. Gravy may be served in a hollowed-out large apple, if desired.
